# Test for testcallgraph tool

# Create a simple test file to analyze
mkdir -p testdir
setstdin exec echo "hello"
exec mcpdiff --help
mcpdiff file1 file2
mcp-spy -- trace.json
exec cat > testdir/simple.txt

# Test basic analysis
exec testcallgraph testdir/simple.txt
stdout 'Program Executions:'
stdout 'echo'
stdout 'mcpdiff'
stdout 'mcp-spy'
stdout 'Call Graph Edges:'

# Test JSON output
exec testcallgraph -format json testdir/simple.txt
stdout '"test_file":'
stdout '"executions":'
stdout '"edges":'

# Test analyze flag
exec testcallgraph -analyze mcpdiff testdir/simple.txt
stdout 'Analysis: mcpdiff'
stdout 'Line 2:'
stdout 'Line 3:'

# Test list-commands
exec testcallgraph -list-commands testdir/simple.txt
stdout 'Custom Commands Found'
stdout 'exec:'
stdout 'mcpdiff:'
stdout 'mcp-spy:'

# Test stats
exec testcallgraph -stats testdir/simple.txt
stdout 'Statistics'
stdout 'Files analyzed: 1'
stdout 'Total executions:'
stdout 'Unique programs:'

# Test dot format
exec testcallgraph -format dot testdir/simple.txt
stdout 'digraph testcallgraph'
stdout 'simple.txt'
stdout 'cmd/echo/main.go:main'
stdout 'cmd/mcpdiff/main.go:main'

# Test with directory
mkdir -p testdir/subdir
exec cp testdir/simple.txt testdir/subdir/another.txt
exec testcallgraph -stats testdir/
stdout 'Files analyzed: 2'

# Test error handling
exec testcallgraph nonexistent.txt
! stdout .
stderr 'Error'

# Test help
exec testcallgraph
stdout 'Usage:'
stdout 'Options:'
stdout 'Examples:'
